title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Verification
verification_method: Planned requirement-home execution, negative transition tests, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase and epoch transitions, BenchmarkDotNet coverage, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.
scope: Verify the future repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle for RFC 9001 Section 6 and Sections 6.1 through 6.6, including local and peer initiated updates, repeated Key Phase alternation, successor packet opening, duplicate and tampered transition rejection, current-phase acknowledgment gating, AEAD usage-limit update requests, old-key retention, and old-key discard synchronized with sender/recovery state. The current artifact records the bounded first-successor retention and discard floors, the helper-backed AEAD protected/opened-packet counter, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification floors, bounded runtime first-update-or-discard send/open wiring, bounded repeated local install after current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, and bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment plus prior old-key discard, but it does not claim repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates, repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor, or endpoint-level stateless-reset-only emission behavior.
preconditions: REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0008 remain the baseline RFC 9001 Section 6 requirements for QUIC Key Phase updates.
REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 define the promoted lifecycle, retention, ordering, and AEAD-limit policy boundary for implementation.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0145 remains the implemented first-successor install prerequisite.

The 9001-06-1rtt-key-update-lifecycle gap remains open until the repeated AEAD-limit-triggered update, broader stateless-reset-only response behavior, and old-key discard requirements beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor are implemented and proven.
procedure: Run the existing first-successor RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests as a regression baseline.
Run the direct RFC 9001 Section 6.1 first-successor requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010.
Run the direct first RFC 9001 Section 6.2 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004.
Run the direct invalid Key Phase RFC 9001 Section 6.3 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001.
Run the direct first next-receive-key RFC 9001 Section 6.3 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002.
Run the direct first send-key ordering RFC 9001 Section 6.4 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003.
Run the direct first old/current next receive-selection RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002.
Run the direct bounded repeated lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive-selection tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001.
Run the direct first retained-old timer and discard RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005.
Run the direct first repeated local-update cooldown RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.
Run the direct bounded repeated local install and outbound Key Phase parity tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003.

Run the direct bounded repeated peer second-successor tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002.
Run the optional `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003` consecutive peer-update policy proof without creating a distinguishable invalid-Key-Phase signal.
Run the direct helper-backed RFC 9001 Section 6.6 AEAD key lifecycle requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` and `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0002`.
Run the direct helper-backed RFC 9001 Section 6.6 AEAD limit policy tests for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0003` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005`.

Add and run positive tests for repeated local-initiated and peer-initiated key updates that alternate the Key Phase bit across more than one epoch only after the confirmation/timing/AEAD-policy subset is in place.

Add and run negative tests for duplicate same-phase packets, stale older-epoch packets, tampered successor packets, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, unacknowledged repeated local updates, premature old-key discard, and authentication failure rollback.

Run the bounded runtime wiring tests that prove AEAD usage-limit decisions install the first local key update before excess protection, discard when successor protection cannot continue, and discard receive paths when integrity limits are reached without sending close datagrams.
Add fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation and packet-protection epoch transition boundaries.
Run a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for successor derivation, epoch install, protected packet formatting, and opening in the repeated lifecycle path.
Render SpecTrace Markdown from JSON and run the repo-local trace validation checks available for the touched artifacts.
expected_result: The eventual lifecycle proof shows that endpoints can repeatedly update 1-RTT packet-protection keys after handshake confirmation, use the Key Phase bit to identify and detect the active epoch, decrypt packets that carry a changed Key Phase, keep both endpoints synchronized, gate local repeated updates on current-phase acknowledgment, handle AEAD usage limits without bypassing that gate, retain old keys long enough for safe reordering, reject packet-number-incoherent old-key packets where required, and discard old 1-RTT material only with sender/recovery cleanup. Until then, this verification remains planned and the repository only claims the bounded first-successor floor, including the first retained-old timer and discard cleanup slice, plus helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded first-update-or-discard runtime wiring, bounded repeated local install/send parity after current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown/retained-old discard, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, and bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment plus prior old-key discard.
status_summary: Planned for the broader repeated lifecycle. On 2026-04-23, the existing first-successor Section 6 regression guard passed 19 focused tests with the serialized `-m:1` run preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-key-update-boundary/. The direct Section 6.1 first-successor proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 with 9 focused tests and a 28-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-first-successor-proof/. The direct Section 6.1 KDF proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0009 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 with 6 focused tests, a 15-test all-S6P1 guard, and a 34-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-kdf-proof/. The direct Section 6.1 old-key retention proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0007 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0008 with 6 focused tests, a 21-test all-S6P1 guard, a 40-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-old-key-retention/. The direct Section 6.2 first peer-update proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002 with 7 focused tests, a 28-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 guard, a 47-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-peer-update-ack/. The direct Section 6.2 consecutive peer-update policy proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003 with 4 focused tests, a 46-test Section 6.1 through Section 6.3 policy guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-consecutive-peer-update-policy/. The direct Section 6.2 old-key ACK proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004 with 4 focused tests, a 55-test first-successor guard spanning Sections 6.1 through 6.5, a 74-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-old-key-ack/. The direct Section 6.3 invalid Key Phase proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001 with 5 focused tests, a 42-test Section 6.1 through Section 6.3 guard, a 93-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p3-invalid-key-phase/. The direct Section 6.3 next receive-key proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 with 5 focused tests, a 33-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 guard, a 52-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p3-next-receive-keys/. The direct Section 6.4 first send-key ordering proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 with 7 focused tests, a 40-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 plus first Section 6.4 guard, and a 59-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 plus first Section 6.4 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p4-send-key-ordering/. The direct Sections 6.4 through 6.5 first old-key receive-selection proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 with 7 focused tests, a 47-test first-successor guard spanning Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 slice, a 66-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-old-key-selection/. The direct Section 6.5 first higher-recovered next-key receive-selection proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 with 4 focused tests, a 51-test first-successor guard spanning Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 receive-selection slice, a 70-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-next-key-selection/. The direct Section 6.5 repeated local-update cooldown proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003 with 6 focused tests, an 88-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-update-cooldown/. The direct Section 6.5 retained-old timer and discard proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 with 8 focused tests, a 63-test first-successor guard spanning Sections 6.1 through 6.5, an 82-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-old-key-discard/. The direct helper-backed Section 6.6 AEAD key lifecycle proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0002 with 8 focused tests, a 31-test AEAD usage-limit guard spanning the existing Appendix B calculator homes plus the new lifecycle homes, and a QuicAeadKeyLifecycleBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p6-aead-key-lifecycle/. The direct Section 6.6 AEAD-limit runtime wiring proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0003 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 with 23 focused S6P6 tests, including first-update-before-excess-protection, successor-protection discard, current and retained-old receive integrity-limit discard, plus a QuicAeadKeyLifecycleBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p6-aead-limit-runtime-wiring/. The direct repeated local install/send parity proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003 with focused S6/S6P5 tests, the S6P5 guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-local-install/. The direct repeated lower-recovered receive-selection proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 with 6 focused S6P5 tests, an 18-test repeated receive-selection guard, a 108-test S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-lower-recovered-selection/. The direct repeated peer second-successor proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 with 28 focused tests, a 116-test S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a 6-case QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-repeated-peer-second-successor/. The touched Markdown views render cleanly; repo-wide SpecTrace core validation still fails at the standing residual baseline outside this slice. The next missing proof set is repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates after the first successor, broader stateless-reset-only response behavior, and repeated old 1-RTT key discard beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor under the gap ledger.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Evidence Boundary
supplemental_sections.content:
- Current tests prove first-successor install, changed-Key Phase observation, tampered-packet rejection, duplicate same-phase rejection, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, first current-phase acknowledgment tracking, the first three-PTO post-confirmation repeated local-update cooldown, bounded repeated local install after cooldown and retained-old discard, outbound Key Phase parity for phase 2, bounded lower-recovered retained phase-1 receive selection after local phase-2 install, bounded peer phase-2 second-successor acceptance after current-phase acknowledgment and old-key discard, per-key-phase current packet-number floor isolation, timing-neutral invalid Key Phase drop classification, timing-neutral no-close handling for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, the first retained-old discard timer after authenticating a new-key packet, the first old 1-RTT read-key discard floor with matching sender/recovery cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, next-Key-Phase guard for successor install, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded runtime first-update-or-discard send/open wiring, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets.
- Current benchmarks cover the first-successor install, repeated local install, repeated peer install, retained phase-1 packet opening after repeated local update, protected packet formatting/opening path, repeated local-update control-state checks, and helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle counter/limit and policy-decision checks.
- No evidence in this artifact claims repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local updates after the first successor, broader stateless-reset-only response behavior, or repeated old 1-RTT key discard beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor.

supplemental_sections.heading: Traffic Secret Advancement Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 traffic-secret advancement run proves that a committed first update advances stored application traffic secrets to the updated `quic ku` secrets and that the helper can derive the next successor packet keys from those advanced secrets.
- The same run preserved the then-current repeated-local-update negative boundary: second-successor material derivation was possible, but repeated local update installation still failed before the current-phase acknowledgment/old-key-discard gate was implemented.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-traffic-secret-advance/` with focused S6P1 tests, the all-S6P1 guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Local Install Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated-local install run proves that a local endpoint can install phase-2 material only after the current phase is acknowledged, the three-PTO cooldown has elapsed, and retained-old first-update material has been discarded.
- The same run proves retained-old material blocks repeated local install, phase-2 protect/open material is installed from advanced traffic secrets, displaced phase-1 material is retained old, and outbound Key Phase emission uses parity so phase 2 writes bit 0.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-local-install/` with focused S6/S6P5 tests, the S6P5 guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Lower-Recovered Receive Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated lower-recovered receive-selection run proves that, after bounded local phase-2 install, the runtime can authenticate lower recovered phase-1 packets using retained previous material.
- The same run proves retained phase-1 material is not accepted when the packet carries the phase-0 bit, isolates the current packet-number floor per installed key phase, and keeps phase-2 current-packet observation from poisoning valid lower recovered phase-1 selection.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-repeated-lower-recovered-selection/` with focused S6P5 tests, the repeated receive-selection guard, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Peer Second-Successor Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- The 2026-04-23 repeated peer second-successor run proves that, after current-phase acknowledgment and prior old-key discard, the runtime can retain second-successor receive material and authenticate peer phase-2 packets with Key Phase bit 0.
- The same run proves phase-2 send keys are installed before ACK emission, repeated higher recovered packet numbers use second-successor keys, and apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates remain timing-neutral drops.
- Evidence is preserved under `artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-repeated-peer-second-successor/` with focused S6P2/S6P3/S6P5 tests, the S6P1-through-S6P6 key-update guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation.

supplemental_sections.heading: Next Planned Proof Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Add repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local update handling or repeated old-key discard cleanup only after their requirement and proof boundaries are traced.
- Keep broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked until its requirements are traced.
